section: Section 2: Accessing WaiverPro — The Landing Page
rule: URL for the landing page is /

section: Section 2: Accessing WaiverPro — The Landing Page
rule: Heading text must be 'Healthcare Waiver Management' with tagline 'Streamline your regulatory waiver process from submission to approval'

section: Section 2: Accessing WaiverPro — The Landing Page
rule: Search box placeholder text must be 'Search waivers' and filters the program list by name

section: Section 2: Accessing WaiverPro — The Landing Page
rule: Each published program card must show its name, description, Processing Time, Waiver Period, and an eligibility checklist

section: Section 2: Accessing WaiverPro — The Landing Page
rule: A program that is not currently open must show a status note (e.g. 'Expired') with the dates it was available

section: Section 2: Accessing WaiverPro — The Landing Page
rule: A 'Sign In' button must appear in the top-right corner and take the user to the sign-in screen

section: Section 2: Accessing WaiverPro — The Landing Page
rule: Footer must contain links to Privacy Policy and Terms of Service, with the line 'Copyright © 2025 WaiverPro'
URL for the sign-in screen is /login
Email field placeholder must be 'm@example.com'
Password must be at least 6 characters
A 'Forgot your password?' link must appear beside the password field
After successful sign-in, the user must be taken to the My Applications dashboard
Signed-in pages share a layout with a fixed sidebar on the left and a header along the top, under URL pattern /dashboard/...
Sidebar top must show brand 'WaiverPro' with label 'Healthcare Waiver Management', followed by primary action button '+ New Waiver Request'
Sidebar navigation items in order must be: My Applications, Facilities, Action Items, User Management, Announcements, Settings
Below a Support heading, four items must appear: FAQs, Tickets, Contact, Take a Tour
Header must contain Search, Notifications (bell icon with badge showing outstanding action items), and Account (initials avatar revealing email and Logout button)
'+ New Waiver Request' button must be available from the sidebar on every page
URL for My Applications dashboard is /dashboard/my-applications
Waiver-type tabs must be: All, Patient Needs Waiver (PNW) Request, Program Flexibility (PF) Request, Workforce Shortage Waiver (WSW) Request
Status chips must be: All Applications, Draft, Submitted, Reopen, In Review, Rejected, Approved, Evaluator Review, Manager Review, Revoked, Expired
Applications table columns must be: Waiver ID, Name, Facility, Type, Status, Created on
Two summary doughnut charts must be present: 'Applications by Type' (PNW, PF, WSW breakdown) and 'Applications Overview' (status breakdown)

section: Section 6: Submitting a New Waiver Application
rule: New Waiver Application opens as a panel over the current page with step tabs: Waiver Type, Select Location, Fill Application

section: Section 6: Submitting a New Waiver Application
rule: Step 1 must list three selectable waiver type cards: Patient Needs Waiver (PNW) Request, Program Flexibility (PF) Request, Workforce Shortage Waiver (WSW) Request

section: Section 6: Submitting a New Waiver Application
rule: A waiver type that is not currently open must be marked (e.g. 'Expired'), cannot be selected, and must show the dates it was available

section: Section 6: Submitting a New Waiver Application
rule: Step 2 (Select Location) requires choosing the facility and facility type

section: Section 6: Submitting a New Waiver Application
rule: Step 3 (Fill Application) requires contact number, dates, description, supporting documents, and a required acknowledgement before submission

section: Section 6: Submitting a New Waiver Application
rule: User must be able to navigate back to an earlier step using Next/back controls, and the panel must close via an × button in the top-right corner
URL for Facilities page is /dashboard/facilities
Status chip filters must be: All Facilities, Due Soon, Upcoming, Scheduled, Overdue, No Status; additional filters by Role, Type, and search by name
Status chips and Status/License Exp Date columns are part of 'Facilities Status Tracking' feature, toggleable under Settings → Features
Facilities table columns must be: Name, Type, License Number, License Exp Date, Status
A 'Facilities Status Overview' summary must appear below the table
URL for Action Items page is /dashboard/action-items
Action Items table columns must be: ID, Name, Status, Priority, Due Date; each Name links to the relevant record
Filterable by Status (values: Done, In Process) and Priority (values: High, Medium, Low)
A summary doughnut chart must show total action items broken down by priority, matching the notification badge count in the header
URL for User Management page is /dashboard/user-management
Two tabs must separate current Members from Pending Invites, each showing a count
User table columns must be: Name, Email, Associated Facilities, Last Updated, Actions (edit and remove)
An 'Invite User' button must appear in the top-right corner to add a new user
URL for Announcements page is /dashboard/announcements
Each announcement must show title, status badge (Active, Upcoming, or Expired), optional priority badge (High, Medium, Low), and date
Announcements must be expandable on click to reveal full detail, and a search box must allow finding specific announcements
URL for FAQs page is /dashboard/faqs; questions must expand on click to reveal answers
URL for Tickets page is /dashboard/tickets, page tagline 'Support Tickets — Track and manage your support tickets'
Tickets listed as cards showing ticket number, status and priority badges, created/updated times; filterable by Status and Priority; 'View Details' opens a ticket; '+ New Ticket' raises one
Create New Support Ticket panel must require Title, Related Application, Category, Sub Category, Description, and allow file attachments, with a 'Create Ticket' button
URL for Contact page is /dashboard/contact, tagline 'Get in touch with our support team'
Support email must be support@waiver-pro.org
Support phone must be +1 (555) 123-4567
Support address must be 123 Healthcare Plaza, Suite 400, New York, NY 10001
Business hours must be Monday – Friday, 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM EST, closed weekends
Messages sent through the contact form are typically answered within 24 hours during business days
URL for Settings page is /dashboard/settings, tagline 'Manage your account and preferences'
Settings organised into four cards: Organisation, Profile Information, Notifications, Security, Features
Profile Information must include first name, last name, read-only email, phone number, with a 'Save Changes' button
Notifications card must have toggles for Email Notifications, Status Updates, and Action Items
Security card requires new password to be at least 6 characters, with a 'Change Password' button
Features card must allow toggling Facilities Status Tracking and FAQ Assistant Chatbot (off by default)
Privacy Policy and Terms of Service must be accessible at /privacy and /terms respectively, without signing in, linked from the landing page footer
Each legal page must show 'Last updated: December 2025' and a 'Back to Home' link
Waiver types must be: Patient Needs Waiver (PNW), Program Flexibility (PF), Workforce Shortage Waiver (WSW)
Application statuses must be: Draft, Submitted, Reopen, In Review, Evaluator Review, Manager Review, Approved, Rejected, Revoked, Expired
Action item statuses must be: Done, In Process; priorities must be High, Medium, Low
Support ticket statuses must be: Open, In Progress, Resolved, Closed; priorities must be High, Medium, Low
Announcement statuses must be: Active (currently in effect), Upcoming (scheduled to take effect), Expired (no longer in effect)
